Ennoblement is the process of reaching a greater state of [[nobility]]. As levels of ennoblement are limited by [[Toposophic level]] traditionally someone is considered to be in a state of nobility when they are at the highest degree of ennoblement they can reach without requiring [[Ascension]].

It is related to the concept of [[Anagoge]] as used by [[John Vervaeke]]<ref>https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jbziEOT2V4c</ref>, although that term is linked to [[advancement]] and [[apotheosis]] too.

== Anagoge ==
Anagoge is the Greek word for the climb towards the metaphorical light that represents a clearer view of reality.<ref name=":0">https://web.archive.org/web/20210307135915/https://awakeningfromthemeaningcrisis.com/anagoge</ref>
[[Category:Nobility]]
[[Category:Ascension]]
[[sn:Ennoblement]][[Category:John Vervaeke]]
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
[[sn:Ennoblement]]The word is closely related to Plato’s cave myth. In this myth, the climb towards reality is what we call anagoge.<ref name=":0" />

== See Also ==

* [[anagoge]]
* [[katagoge]]

== References ==
[[Category:Nobility]]
[[Category:Ascension]]
[[sn:Ennoblement]][[Category:Ennoblement]]
[[Category:Pages]]
